137.reason: [The error message is hardcoded to always output error code 'a', but the line number recorded may be incorrect because `lineNumber` is reset in `clearToken()` before processing each token. This causes the reported line number to be 0 or stale, leading to wrong error output format.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The error message always uses error code 'a', but the line number recorded may be incorrect because `lineNumber` is reset in `clearToken()` before processing each token, causing loss of current line context.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The error message is hardcoded to always output error code 'a', but the lineNumber used in error() may be incorrect because lineNumber is reset to 0 in clearToken() before processing each token, leading to wrong line number in error report.]

299.reason: [The error category code is hardcoded as "a", but the lineNumber used here may be incorrect because when encountering a single '&' or '|' at the end of a line, the lexer might have already advanced to the next line before detecting the error. Additionally, in the case where the erroneous symbol appears on a new line that was just read, lineNumber has been incremented, but the symbol actually belongs to that new line—this part is correct. However, the real issue is that when the lexer reads past the current line while processing tokens like comments or strings, the lineNumber may not reflect the actual line of the lexical error. But more critically: the error() method always appends error code "a", which is correct per spec for isolated & or |, BUT the main bug is that when a single '&' or '|' appears at the very end of file (i.e., after last newline), lineNumber might be 0 or incorrect due to clearToken() resetting lineNumber to 0.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The `lineNumber` used here may be incorrect because `clearToken()` resets `lineNumber` to 0 at the start of each `next()` call, but `lineNumber` should reflect the actual line where the token (and thus the error) occurs. The correct line number is updated only when reading a new line, but resetting it in `clearToken()` destroys that state.]

Additionally, in the case where the erroneous symbol appears on a new line that was just read, lineNumber has been incremented, but the symbol actually belongs to that new line—this part is correct. However, the real issue is that when the lexer reads past the current line while processing tokens like comments or strings, the lineNumber may not reflect the actual line of the lexical error. But more critically: the error() method always appends error code "a", which is correct per spec for isolated & or |, BUT the main bug is that when a single '&' or '|' appears at the very end of file (i.e., after last newline), lineNumber might be 0 or incorrect due to clearToken() resetting lineNumber to 0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Uses hardcoded error code 'a' which is correct per spec for single '&' or '|', but `lineNumber` might be 0 due to being cleared in `clearToken()`, leading to wrong line number in output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The lineNumber here may be 0 or incorrect because clearToken() resets lineNumber to 0 at the start of each next() call, but lineNumber should reflect the actual source line where the error occurred.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The error category code is hardcoded as "a", but the lineNumber used here may be incorrect because when encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer has already advanced to the next character and possibly consumed part of the next token; more critically, the lineNumber might not reflect the actual line where the erroneous symbol appears due to how currentLine and lineNumber are updated in next(). Specifically, if the erroneous '&' or '|' is at the start of a new line, lineNumber may have been incremented prematurely before processing the character.]
